title: India-China Relations Normalising: Top Officials Meet in Delhi
summary: Senior Indian and Chinese officials met in New Delhi, with India stating that bilateral ties are normalising after years of strain following the 2020 Galwan Valley clash. The meeting signals continued diplomatic engagement as part of the disengagement process along the Line of Actual Control. Both sides have been working to restore normalcy in diplomatic, trade, and people-to-people relations.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's foreign policy priorities, border management, and the ongoing LAC disengagement process; connects to GS Paper 2 topics on India's neighbourhood policy and bilateral diplomacy.
key_facts: India described ties with China as 'normalising'
Meeting held in New Delhi between top officials
Context: Post-2020 Galwan Valley military standoff
Part of ongoing LAC disengagement and diplomatic reset

title: NSA Ajit Doval Holds Talks with Iran's Top Security Official Amid Middle East Tensions
summary: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval met with Iran's top security official to discuss ongoing Middle East tensions. The talks come amid the US-Iran nuclear/peace negotiations and Iran's threats regarding the Strait of Hormuz. India has strategic interests in the region due to its energy imports, diaspora in Gulf countries, and the Chabahar Port project.
exam_angle: Reflects India's diplomatic engagement in West Asia, energy security interests, and the importance of the Strait of Hormuz for global oil trade; connects to India's 'Act West' policy.
key_facts: NSA Ajit Doval met Iran's top security official
Context: Ongoing US-Iran talks and Middle East tensions
Strait of Hormuz is critical for ~20% of global oil trade
India has strategic interest via Chabahar Port and energy imports from Gulf

title: US Authorises Iranian Oil Sales, Eases Sanctions Amid Nuclear Deal Talks
summary: The United States authorised Iranian oil sales and eased sanctions on Iranian oil exports as negotiations toward a final peace/nuclear deal progressed. Mediators reported 'progress' after a lengthy opening session. The US Vice President JD Vance stated that unfrozen Iranian funds would not be used to finance terrorism. A $300 billion fund structure is reportedly at the heart of the deal.
exam_angle: Critical for understanding global oil market dynamics, India's energy security (Iran was once a top oil supplier to India), and geopolitical implications of a US-Iran nuclear deal on West Asia stability.
key_facts: US eased sanctions on Iranian oil exports
US-Iran nuclear/peace deal talks showing 'progress'
$300 billion fund reportedly at heart of proposed deal
Iran had previously threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz

title: Iran Claims Strait of Hormuz Closed; Tracking Data Shows Ships Still Crossing
summary: Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed, though vessel tracking data continued to show ships transiting the waterway. The claim came amid heightened Middle East tensions and ongoing US-Iran nuclear negotiations. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical global chokepoint through which approximately one-fifth of the world's oil supply passes.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz is a key chokepoint in global energy trade; disruption directly affects India's oil imports and energy security — a recurring theme in GS Paper 3 (Economy/Energy) and GS Paper 2 (IR).
key_facts: Strait of Hormuz: located between Iran and Oman
~20% of global oil supply transits through it
Iran claimed closure but ship tracking showed transit continuing
Critical for India's crude oil import routes

title: India in Talks to Sell BrahMos Supersonic Missile to UAE; Akashteer Defence System Also Discussed
summary: India is reportedly in talks to sell the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile to the United Arab Emirates, marking a potential major defence export deal. The Akashteer automated air defence control and reporting system was also mentioned in the context of defence cooperation. An Israeli defence delegation also visited India during the same period to deepen the strategic partnership.
exam_angle: Connects to India's growing defence exports ambition (target: $5 billion by 2025), 'Make in India' in defence, and bilateral strategic partnerships in West Asia; BrahMos is a joint venture with Russia.
key_facts: BrahMos: India-Russia joint venture supersonic cruise missile
BrahMos speed: ~2.8 Mach; range ~290-800 km depending on variant
Akashteer: Automated Air Defence Control & Reporting System developed by DRDO/BEL
UAE is a key strategic partner in India's 'Act West' policy

title: India's Core Sector Growth Slows to Seven-Month Low of 0.5% in May 2026
summary: India's eight core sector industries recorded a growth of only 0.5% in May 2026, the slowest in seven months. The core sector comprises eight industries — coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilisers, steel, cement, and electricity — which together account for about 40% of the Index of Industrial Production (IIP). The slowdown signals potential moderation in overall industrial output.
exam_angle: Core sector data is a leading indicator for IIP; directly relevant to GS Paper 3 topics on Indian economy, industrial growth, and economic indicators used in policy assessment.
key_facts: Core sector growth: 0.5% in May 2026 (seven-month low)
Eight core industries account for ~40% of IIP weight
Eight sectors: coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilisers, steel, cement, electricity
Published by: Ministry of Commerce & Industry

title: India Imposes Anti-Dumping Duty on Rubber Chemical Imports from China, EU, and US
summary: India imposed anti-dumping duties on imports of rubber chemicals from China, the European Union, and the United States. Anti-dumping duties are levied when foreign goods are sold in India below their normal value, causing injury to domestic industry. The decision is in line with India's broader trade defence measures to protect domestic manufacturers.
exam_angle: Anti-dumping measures are governed by the WTO Anti-Dumping Agreement and enforced by the Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR) in India; relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy/Trade Policy).
key_facts: Anti-dumping duty imposed on rubber chemical imports
Countries affected: China, European Union, USA
Administered by: Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR), Ministry of Commerce
WTO allows anti-dumping measures under Article VI of GATT

title: India Seeks Preferential Tariff Access in Push to Finalise US Trade Deal
summary: India is seeking tariff advantages over competing nations as it pushes to finalise a bilateral trade agreement with the United States. Indian trade minister confirmed that India is seeking preferential market access through the deal. Farm organisations and trade unions separately wrote to PM Modi opposing the FTA with the US, citing concerns over agricultural sector impacts.
exam_angle: India-US trade relations are a major GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy) topic; FTA negotiations, tariff structures, and domestic opposition reflect the complexity of trade policy.
key_facts: India seeking preferential tariff access in US trade deal
Farm outfits and trade unions opposed the India-US FTA
India-UK FTA also nearing finalisation (July 15 rollout reported)
UK's PM Keir Starmer resigned amid India-UK trade deal progress

title: Rupee Falls 30 Paise to 94.63 Against Dollar Amid Middle East Uncertainty
summary: The Indian rupee depreciated by 30 paise to trade at 94.63 against the US dollar, driven by uncertainty stemming from Middle East tensions, particularly Iran's threats to close the Strait of Hormuz. A weaker rupee raises India's import bill, especially for crude oil, and affects inflation dynamics. The movement reflects global risk-off sentiment.
exam_angle: Exchange rate movements, their causes (current account deficit, oil prices, capital flows), and impacts on the Indian economy are standard GS Paper 3 (Economy) topics.
key_facts: Rupee fell 30 paise to 94.63 per US dollar
Trigger: Middle East uncertainty and Iran-Hormuz tensions
India imports ~85% of its crude oil needs
Managed by RBI through foreign exchange intervention

title: India Becomes World's Top Ship Recycling Nation in 2025, Achieving Vision 2030 Target Early
summary: India emerged as the world's leading ship recycling nation in 2025, achieving its Vision 2030 target five years ahead of schedule. The Alang ship-breaking yard in Gujarat is the largest ship recycling facility in the world and a key driver of this achievement. Ship recycling contributes significantly to India's steel sector through the recovery of scrap metal.
exam_angle: Connects to India's maritime sector, 'Make in India', circular economy, and the Hong Kong International Convention for the Safe and Environmentally Sound Recycling of Ships; relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy/Environment).
key_facts: India ranked #1 in global ship recycling in 2025
Vision 2030 target achieved 5 years ahead of schedule
Alang (Gujarat) is the world's largest ship recycling yard
Governed internationally by the Hong Kong Convention (2009)

title: Tata Electronics Hit by Cyber Breach Allegedly Exposing Apple and Tesla Trade Secrets
summary: Tata Electronics, a major supplier to Apple and Tesla, suffered a cyber security breach in which attackers claimed to have accessed and exposed sensitive trade secrets related to both companies. Tata Electronics manufactures iPhones in India and has been a key player in India's electronics manufacturing ecosystem. The breach raises concerns about supply chain cyber security.
exam_angle: Cybersecurity of critical manufacturing infrastructure, data protection laws, and India's role in global electronics supply chains are relevant GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech/Economy) themes.
key_facts: Tata Electronics is a key Apple iPhone manufacturer in India
Breach claimed to expose Apple and Tesla trade secrets
India's IT Act 2000 and DPDP Act 2023 govern data protection
Incident highlights supply chain cybersecurity vulnerabilities

title: Supreme Court Rules Right to Walk on Footpaths is a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to walk on footpaths constitutes a fundamental right of citizens. The court held that encroachment of footpaths by hawkers, illegal structures, or vehicles violates citizens' constitutional rights. The judgment is significant for urban governance and the rights of pedestrians in Indian cities.
exam_angle: Expands the interpretation of fundamental rights (likely Articles 19 and 21); relevant to GS Paper 2 (Polity) on judicial activism, fundamental rights, and urban local body governance.
key_facts: Supreme Court declared right to walk on footpaths a fundamental right
Relevant constitutional provisions: Articles 19 and 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Connects to earlier SC judgments on right to livelihood vs. right to public space
Implications for urban local body encroachment removal drives

title: Supreme Court Refuses to Entertain Plea Against Collegium Picks for High Court Judgeship
summary: The Supreme Court refused to entertain a plea filed by a Himachal Pradesh judicial officer challenging the Collegium's recommendations for High Court judgeships. The refusal reaffirms the Supreme Court's position on the primacy of the Collegium system in judicial appointments. The Collegium system, established through the Second, Third, and Fourth Judges cases, remains the mechanism for appointment of judges to the Supreme Court and High Courts.
exam_angle: The Collegium system for judicial appointments is a critical GS Paper 2 (Polity) topic; connects to NJAC judgment (2015), independence of judiciary, and separation of powers.
key_facts: SC refused to entertain challenge against Collegium's HC judge recommendations
Collegium system established through Second Judges Case (1993) and Third Judges Case (1998)
NJAC Act struck down by SC in 2015 (5-0 majority)
Collegium comprises CJI + 4 senior-most SC judges for apex court appointments

title: Plea Filed in Supreme Court Seeking CBI Probe into Ram Temple Fund Irregularities
summary: A plea was filed in the Supreme Court seeking registration of an FIR and a CBI-led investigation into alleged financial irregularities in the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, which manages the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. The petition sought a court-monitored probe into alleged embezzlement of funds collected for the temple's construction. The Supreme Court listed the matter for hearing.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 (Polity) regarding judicial oversight of trusts, PIL jurisdiction, and the role of constitutional courts in accountability; also connects to places in news (Ayodhya).
key_facts: Plea filed seeking CBI probe into Ram Temple fund irregularities
Trust involved: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust
Temple located in Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh (consecrated January 2024)
Supreme Court PIL jurisdiction under Article 32

title: Meta Invests $900 Million in CRED; Appoints Founder Kunal Shah as WhatsApp CEO
summary: Meta announced a $900 million investment in Indian fintech startup CRED and appointed its founder Kunal Shah as the new CEO of WhatsApp, making him the first Indian to head the world's largest messaging platform with over 2 billion users. This marks a significant milestone for India's startup ecosystem and reflects growing global recognition of Indian tech entrepreneurs. CRED is known for its credit card payment and rewards platform.
exam_angle: Relevant as a 'Person in News' item (Kunal Shah); also connects to India's fintech ecosystem, digital payments, and foreign investment in Indian startups — GS Paper 3 themes.
key_facts: Kunal Shah: Founder of CRED, appointed WhatsApp CEO
Meta invested $900 million in CRED
WhatsApp has ~2 billion+ global users; ~500 million in India
First Indian founder to lead WhatsApp globally

title: Alan Greenspan, Longest-Serving US Federal Reserve Chair, Dies at 100
summary: Alan Greenspan, the longest-serving Chairman of the US Federal Reserve (1987–2006), passed away at the age of 100. He served under four US presidents and presided over periods of economic expansion as well as crises including the 1987 stock market crash and the dot-com bubble. His policies significantly influenced global monetary economics and the concept of central bank independence.
exam_angle: Relevant as a 'Person in News'; Greenspan's tenure connects to GS Paper 3 topics on central banking, monetary policy, and the role of the US Fed in global economic stability.
key_facts: Alan Greenspan: US Federal Reserve Chairman 1987–2006 (longest-serving)
Served under Presidents Reagan, Bush Sr., Clinton, and Bush Jr.
Known for the 'Greenspan Put' and 'irrational exuberance' phrase (1996)
Died at age 100

title: Five Eyes Alliance Warns AI Models Capable of Threatening Governments 'Months Away'
summary: The Five Eyes intelligence alliance (USA, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand) issued a rare joint statement warning that AI models capable of taking down governments and major businesses are 'months, not years' away. The statement highlighted the urgent need for AI preparedness across national security, critical infrastructure, and governance frameworks. This is among the first such collective AI security warnings from the alliance.
exam_angle: Connects to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) on AI governance, cybersecurity, and emerging technology threats; also relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) on multilateral security arrangements like Five Eyes.
key_facts: Five Eyes: Intelligence alliance of USA, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand
Warning: Dangerous AI capabilities 'months, not years' away
Concerns include threats to critical infrastructure and governance
Rare joint public statement on AI security preparedness

title: Tamil Nadu Ranks 2nd in India for Hot-Humid Days: Climate Report
summary: A report highlighted that Tamil Nadu ranks second in India in terms of the number of hot-humid days experienced annually. This data underscores the state's vulnerability to heat stress, particularly for outdoor workers and urban populations. The finding is relevant in the context of India's National Action Plan on Climate Change and heat action plans at the state level.
exam_angle: Connects to GS Paper 3 (Environment) on climate change adaptation, heat action plans, and the Wet Bulb Temperature concept; also relevant to disaster management frameworks.
key_facts: Tamil Nadu ranks 2nd in India for hot-humid days
Hot-humid conditions assessed via Wet Bulb Globe Temperature (WBGT)
India's National Heat Action Plan aims to reduce heat-related mortality
Rajasthan/Telangana face dry heat; coastal states face humid heat stress

title: Bioenergy Push: Minister Directs Officials to Expand Non-Bagasse Projects
summary: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi directed officials to step up India's bioenergy efforts and expand projects beyond bagasse-based bioenergy to include other biomass sources. India's National Bioenergy Programme aims to utilise agricultural residue, municipal solid waste, and other non-conventional biomass for energy generation. Diversification beyond bagasse is critical for year-round bioenergy availability.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Environment/Energy) on India's renewable energy targets, National Bioenergy Programme, and reducing crop residue burning; connects to PM-KUSUM and biofuel policies.
key_facts: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) oversees bioenergy
Bagasse: sugarcane residue, currently dominant bioenergy feedstock
National Bioenergy Programme: umbrella scheme for biomass, biogas, waste-to-energy
India's bioenergy target: 10 GW by 2030 under National Bioenergy Programme
